message:
fix(web): handle SharedArrayBuffer in TextDecoder calls across workers

TextDecoder.decode() throws when given a Uint8Array view backed by a
SharedArrayBuffer. This manifests in Chrome when SharedSequencePool
passes shared memory to workers.

Fix: Check if buffer is SharedArrayBuffer, copy to regular Uint8Array
before decoding. Applied consistently to:
- SharedSequencePool.ts
- analysis.worker.ts
- comparison.worker.ts
- dotplot.worker.ts

Also fixes ScrollProvider lint errors (unused underscore-prefixed vars
that should have been used in shouldEnable calculation).

Closes: phage_explorer-ny6p

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-2dzx","title":"Feature: Interaction Feedback Enhancement - Tactile Responses","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:36:00.722039133Z","updated_at":"2025-12-13T02:10:15.897748349Z","closed_at":"2025-12-13T02:10:15.897748349Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-2dzx","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-vntd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:36:54.541569438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-2dzx","author":"ubuntu","text":"# Interaction Feedback Enhancement - Tactile Responses

## The Problem
Buttons and interactive elements in Phage Explorer lack the "pop" and tactile feel of premium apps. Interactions feel flat and unresponsive.

## Current State (components.css:823-838)
- Hover: background color change
- Active: only transform
- No visual "pop" on click
- No scale effect
- Transitions vary (inconsistent timing)

## Premium App Comparison

### Linear
- Instant color change on press
- Subtle scale (0.98) on active
- 100ms transition (fast)
- Consistent across all buttons

### Stripe Dashboard
- Shadow change on hover
- Scale + shadow on active
- Smooth 150ms transitions
- Consistent design tokens

### Our Target
- Immediate visual response (< 50ms)
- Consistent timing across all interactions
- Scale + color + shadow combined
- Satisfying "click" feel

## Solution

### 1. Unified Timing Tokens
```css
:root {
  --transition-instant: 50ms;
  --transition-fast: 100ms;
  --transition-normal: 200ms;
  --ease-out-expo: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
}
```

### 2. Button Feedback States
```css
.btn {
  transition: 
    background-color var(--transition-instant),
    transform var(--transition-fast) var(--ease-out-expo),
    box-shadow var(--transition-fast);
}

.btn:hover {
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15);
}

.btn:active {
  transform: scale(0.97);
  box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
}
```

### 3. List Item Selection
```css
.list-item {
  transition: background-color var(--transition-instant);
}

.list-item:active {
  transform: scale(0.99);
  background-color: var(--color-accent-10);
}

.list-item.selected {
  box-shadow: inset 3px 0 0 var(--color-accent);
}
```

### 4. Overlay Backdrop
```css
.overlay-backdrop {
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background-color var(--transition-fast);
}

.overlay-backdrop:hover {
  background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6); /* Slightly darker */
}
```

## Files to Modify
- `packages/web/src/styles/variables.css` - Add timing tokens
- `packages/web/src/styles/components.css` - Update button/list styles
- `packages/web/src/components/overlays/Overlay.tsx` - Backdrop hover

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All buttons have immediate visual feedback
- [ ] Consistent timing across app
- [ ] Scale effect on active state
- [ ] List items have selection feedback
- [ ] Overlay backdrop responds to hover","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:37:57Z"},{"id":51,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-2dzx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Closed remaining child tasks: timing tokens already exist in variables.css, list-item selection feedback already implemented in components.css, and button active/hover now has scale + shadow feedback (box-shadow via index.css, scale already in components.css). Closing feature as complete.","created_at":"2025-12-13T02:10:05Z"}]}

+{"id":"phage_explorer-2k9e","title":"Fix TextDecoder crash with SharedArrayBuffer views in WASM workers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:49:55.633230738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:50:08.246104899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:50:08.246040328Z","close_reason":"Fixed. TextDecoder.decode() rejects SharedArrayBuffer views per WHATWG spec. Added check in decodeAsciiBytes() and decodeSequence() to copy shared views to regular Uint8Array before decoding. Fixed in 4 files: analysis.worker.ts, SharedSequencePool.ts, comparison.worker.ts, dotplot.worker.ts. This resolves Bias Decomposition, Phase Portrait, k-mer, and dot plot analysis failures on systems with COOP/COEP headers enabled.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-2moc","title":"Fix React Query cache settings - prevent unbounded memory growth","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:11:20.501873046Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T16:34:25.550236659Z","closed_at":"2025-12-15T16:34:25.550236659Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-2moc","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-qjmq","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:11:20.508127063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":57,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-2moc","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Fix React Query Cache Settings

### Problem
In `packages/web/src/queryClient.ts` lines 6-7:
```typescript
staleTime: Infinity,
gcTime: Infinity,
```

This configuration causes **unbounded memory growth** because:
- `staleTime: Infinity` means data never becomes stale (acceptable for our use case)
- `gcTime: Infinity` (formerly cacheTime) means data is **NEVER garbage collected**

Over a 30+ minute session browsing multiple phages, cached query results accumulate indefinitely, eventually causing browser sluggishness or OOM.

### Solution
Change to reasonable TTLs that still provide good UX:
```typescript
staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,  // 5 minutes - data stays fresh for typical session
gcTime: 10 * 60 * 1000,    // 10 minutes - GC after not accessed for 10min
```

### Considerations
- 5 minutes staleTime is generous - phage data doesn't change
- 10 minutes gcTime allows background phages to persist briefly
- If user returns to a phage within 10 min, it's still in cache
- After 10 min of disuse, it's freed - memory bounded

### Testing
1. Open DevTools > Memory tab
2. Browse through all 12 phages multiple times
3. Wait 15 minutes
4. Heap should stabilize (not grow indefinitely)

### Impact
- Prevents memory leaks in long sessions
- No visible UX impact (data refetches are fast from local SQLite)","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:11:42Z"}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-nm9","title":"Advanced: High-Resolution K-mer Anomaly Cartography","description":"Map localized k-mer rarity via z-scores vs reference corpus. Sliding window with Welford variance + LOF. Fuse with GC skew and coding density. Scrolling heat-strip under sequence grid; hotspots pulse; side panel shows top k-mers and genes. Flags HGT/AMG/defense islands. Novelty: Medium-high.","notes":"## Current Implementation Status

**KmerAnomalyOverlay.tsx is functional:**
- Chi-squared deviation from global k-mer distribution
- Sliding window analysis (500bp windows, 100bp step)
- Interactive heatmap with click-to-inspect
- Configurable k-mer size (3-6)
- Top anomalous k-mers per region displayed
- Color-coded deviation visualization

**Remaining for full vision:**
1. **Reference corpus comparison** - Currently self-referential (window vs whole genome). Full implementation needs precomputed corpus means/variances.
2. **Welford streaming algorithm** - Current is batch; streaming would help with very large genomes
3. **LOF (Local Outlier Factor)** - Would add neighborhood-based anomaly detection
4. **Composite score** - Combine with GC skew + coding density (requires integrating other analysis modules)
5. **Rust+WASM acceleration** - For performance on large genomes

**Assessment:** Core overlay is functional and useful. Advanced features are P3 nice-to-haves.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T05:17:45.217066Z","updated_at":"2026-01-08T22:45:52.024596Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T20:04:34.296324461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-nm9","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T03:01:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-o530","title":"Integrate react-use-gesture for native-feeling touch interactions","description":"# Feature: Native Touch Interactions with react-use-gesture

## Problem Statement
Current touch interactions feel "web-like" rather than native. Missing:
- Swipe gestures with momentum and spring physics
- Pinch-to-zoom with proper bounds and inertia
- Long-press context menus
- Pull-to-refresh patterns

## Library Selection

### react-use-gesture (Recommended)
- React-first API with hooks
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Works with react-spring for physics
- 6.5kb gzipped
- Actively maintained

### Alternative: Hammer.js
- Framework-agnostic
- Larger bundle (7.4kb)
- Less React-idiomatic

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Install Dependencies
npm install @use-gesture/react @react-spring/web

### 2. Create useGestures Hook
packages/web/src/hooks/useGestures.ts

- Unified gesture handling
- Configurable thresholds
- Haptic feedback integration
- Reduced motion support

### 3. Implement Specific Gestures

#### Phage Navigation (Horizontal Swipe)
- Swipe left → next phage
- Swipe right → prev phage
- Momentum/spring animation
- Resistance at list bounds

#### Sequence Zoom (Pinch)
- Pinch to zoom sequence view
- Double-tap to reset zoom
- Smooth interpolation

#### Bottom Sheet Drag
- Drag down to dismiss
- Snap points (half, full, closed)
- Velocity-based snap decision

#### Long Press
- Long press on gene → context menu
- Long press on amino acid → details popup

### 4. Add Spring Animations
Animate gesture-driven state changes:
- Sheet positions
- Card slides
- Zoom levels

## Files to Create/Modify
- packages/web/src/hooks/useGestures.ts (new)
- packages/web/src/hooks/useSwipeNavigation.ts (new)
- packages/web/src/components/mobile/BottomSheet.tsx (enhance)
- packages/web/src/hooks/useSequenceGrid.ts (enhance pinch)
- packages/web/src/App.tsx (add swipe navigation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Swipe navigation feels like native iOS/Android app
- [ ] Pinch zoom has smooth spring animation
- [ ] Bottom sheet drags and snaps naturally
- [ ] Long press triggers context menus
- [ ] All gestures respect prefers-reduced-motion
- [ ] Haptic feedback on successful gestures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:00:56.305281893Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T00:05:15.274104933Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T00:05:15.274104933Z","close_reason":"Integrated @use-gesture/react and @react-spring/web. Created useGestures/useSwipeNavigation hooks. Enhanced BottomSheet with spring physics.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-o530","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-bmmb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:01:22.304791152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-o5y7","title":"Web mobile: 3D fullscreen shows black screen","description":"## User-facing problem
On mobile, tapping the maximize/fullscreen control in the 3D structure viewer results in the entire screen going effectively **black** (HUD shows, but the model is not visible / rendering appears paused).

## Likely root causes
1) **IntersectionObserver threshold bug**: the 3D viewer render loop is gated by an IntersectionObserver using `threshold: 0.05`. If WebKit fails to fire a threshold crossing during fullscreen/layout transitions, `isInViewportRef` can remain false and the render loop never resumes (canvas stays at cleared background).
2) **iOS fixed-position containing block**: the CSS fullscreen fallback uses `position: fixed` (`.three-container--fullscreen:not(:fullscreen)`), but iOS-specific CSS applies transforms to `.app-shell` which can unintentionally change the containing block for fixed elements and/or interact badly with `overflow: hidden` on the shell.
3) **Resize / DPR / context loss**: fullscreen can trigger a large resize; on some mobile GPUs the WebGL context can be lost or fail to repaint unless we explicitly handle it (and force a render tick after resize).

## Goals
- Fullscreen/maximized 3D mode shows the model reliably on iOS Safari and Chrome Android.
- Entering/leaving fullscreen always triggers a render + resize to correct dimensions.
- No black screen regressions; if WebGL context is lost, show a clear error with retry.

## Proposed implementation
- Change the 3D IntersectionObserver to use `threshold: 0` (resume as soon as any pixel is visible), matching the sequence viewer’s strategy.
- When `model3DFullscreen` becomes true, force `isInViewportRef.current = true` and call `requestRender()` after a `requestAnimationFrame()` to allow layout to settle.
- For iOS CSS fullscreen fallback, ensure no parent transform blocks `position: fixed` behavior (e.g., toggle a document-level class while fullscreen that disables the `.app-shell` transform used for other scroll fixes).
- Optionally listen for `webglcontextlost`/`webglcontextrestored` and surface a retry UI if the context is lost.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] On an iPhone, tapping fullscreen shows the model (not just HUD) and the user can orbit/zoom.
- [ ] On Android Chrome, fullscreen works and resizes correctly on orientation change.
- [ ] Exiting fullscreen returns to the in-page viewer without broken layout or stuck rendering.

## Test plan
- Unit: mock IntersectionObserver callback and ensure fullscreen forces a render request.
- E2E (later): Playwright mobile emulation clicks fullscreen and asserts the WebGL canvas has non-uniform pixels (not a solid fill).
- Manual QA: verify on real iOS Safari (since fullscreen + WebGL often differs from emulation).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-13T07:10:32.324009135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T07:38:45.321293677Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T07:38:45.321293677Z","close_reason":"Fixed: IO threshold 0 + fullscreen render kick + iOS shell-transform override for CSS fullscreen fallback","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-o5y7","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-13T07:10:32.329246410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx","title":"Phase 7: Premium UX polish refresh (2026)","description":"## Why this exists
A prior “Stripe-level” audit and polish pass was executed (see `phage_explorer-vntd`, closed 2025-12-13). Since then, new UI surfaces landed and some discoverability + consistency drift reappeared. This epic captures a **fresh-eyes, high-signal set of remaining + newly discovered polish work** for Phase 7.

## High-level goal
Make the product feel:
- **Professional**: consistent visual language, predictable interactions, excellent empty/error/loading states.
- **Fast**: perceived performance and responsiveness even during heavy compute.
- **Trustworthy**: no mismatched shortcuts, no confusing controls, clear mental model.
- **Accessible**: keyboard-first and screen-reader-friendly, with reduced-motion support.

## Key findings (root causes)
1) **Single-source-of-truth drift for shortcuts**
   - Help/shortcut UI can fall out of sync with actual `useHotkey` bindings.
   - Root cause: shortcuts are defined in multiple places (hook usage, overlay code, help overlay docs).

2) **Overlay lazy-load vs hotkey registration paradox**
   - Overlays are `React.lazy` (good for bundle + startup), but hotkeys often register inside overlay components.
   - If we only mount overlays when opened, global shortcuts are missing until first open.
   - If we mount all overlays for hotkeys, we lose lazy-load benefits.
   - Root cause: hotkey registration is coupled to overlay component mount.

3) **Design-system erosion**
   - Inline styles and one-off CSS patterns reduce consistency, slow down future polish, and increase “paper cut” UI bugs.
   - Root cause: no enforced set of primitives/tokens for overlay chrome, typography, spacing.

4) **Typography mismatch**
   - Global monospace in the web UI makes the product feel like a dev tool.
   - Root cause: typography baseline chooses mono everywhere instead of mono-for-data.

5) **Motion/FX defaults**
   - Background FX can read as gimmicky or distract from analysis; must be subtle and respect device constraints.
   - Root cause: FX toggles/defaults not guided by a clear “premium” motion policy.

6) **Keyboard/focus handling inconsistency**
   - Some components use ad-hoc `window.addEventListener('keydown')` patterns.
   - Root cause: missing central conventions for where hotkeys live, how focus disables hotkeys, etc.

## Success criteria (epic-level)
- Help overlay and any shortcut UI is **always correct** (derived from a single registry).
- Overlays remain truly lazy-loaded while global hotkeys stay available.
- Overlay chrome feels consistent: header, actions, padding, spacing, typography, close affordances.
- Default aesthetic is “quiet premium”: less “terminal gimmick”, more analysis-first clarity.
- Reduced-motion users have a pleasant experience; heavy FX never degrade usability.

## Execution model
This epic is executed via sub-epics:
- Hotkeys + Help (registry + drift prevention)
- Overlay architecture (true lazy overlays + global hotkeys)
- Web design system (tokens, typography, primitives)
- Motion/FX policy + defaults
- Loading/empty/error polish
- Keyboard/focus conventions and cleanups

Each sub-epic includes acceptance criteria and small incremental steps (avoid sweeping refactors).

All Phase 7 success criteria met.","compaction_level":0,"labels":["polish","tui","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-vntd","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-06T01:45:33.147427192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-wzjl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-06T01:45:27.922979319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1","title":"Hotkeys + Help: single source of truth","description":"## Problem
Keyboard shortcuts are a core promise of Phage Explorer (see README “Depth Layers & Keybinding Conventions”). Today, shortcut discoverability can drift:
- Help/shortcut UI is sometimes **hardcoded** and can become incorrect as `useHotkey` bindings evolve.
- Users lose trust quickly when “?” shows wrong shortcuts.

## Root cause
Shortcut definitions live in multiple places:
- `useHotkey` calls (actual behavior)
- overlay UI (documentation)
- docs tables (README)

Without a shared registry, drift is inevitable.

## Goal
Create a **single source of truth** for actions + hotkeys and use it to drive:
- Help overlay shortcut table
- Command palette / menus / overlay hints
- (Optional) docs generation later

## Scope
Web first; ensure the design supports TUI parity later.

## Proposed approach
- Define an `ActionRegistry` (or equivalent) with:
  - `actionId` (stable)
  - `title`, `category`, `description`
  - `defaultShortcut` (and alternates)
  - `context` (global vs when overlay open)
  - `surface` (Web/TUI)
- Update the help overlay to render from the registry (no hardcoded lists).
- Add tests that:
  - ensure every global hotkey is present in the registry
  - ensure help overlay renders registry content (smoke)
  - prevent duplicate/conflicting shortcuts at startup

## Acceptance criteria
- Help overlay lists shortcuts that match reality.
- Adding/changing a shortcut requires editing **one place**.
- A test fails if shortcuts drift.

## Notes / likely touchpoints
- `packages/web/src/components/overlays/HelpOverlay.tsx`
- `packages/web/src/hooks/useHotkey.ts` (or equivalent)
- `packages/web/src/components/overlays/OverlayManager.tsx`

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.1","title":"ActionRegistry foundation (ActionId + metadata)","description":"## Goal
Create the minimal **ActionRegistry** that can serve as the single source of truth for action metadata and default shortcuts.

## Requirements
- Define stable `ActionId` values (string literal union / const map).
- Registry entries include:
  - `id`, `title`, `category`
  - optional `description`
  - `defaultShortcut` (single or array)
  - `scope`: `global` vs `contextual`
  - optional `surfaces`: `web`/`tui`

## Design constraints
- Keep it lightweight (no big framework).
- Prefer locating it near existing keyboard/hotkey code.
- Avoid file sprawl: 1 new file max unless there’s a clear existing home.

## Acceptance criteria
- Registry can be imported without pulling overlay bundles.
- Types prevent missing fields and invalid IDs.

## Notes
This task intentionally does *not* change behavior yet; it creates the data model other tasks depend on.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.2","title":"Wire useHotkey to registry + focus rules","description":"## Goal
Ensure actual hotkey bindings and the ActionRegistry stay synchronized.

## Approach
- Update `useHotkey` (or the hotkey binding layer) to:
  - require an `ActionId` when registering a hotkey
  - optionally validate that the ActionRegistry entry exists
  - apply consistent focus rules (do not fire while typing unless allowed)

## Considerations
- Avoid coupling to overlay component mount; this will be handled more fully in the overlay-architecture sub-epic.
- Keep runtime validation dev-only if needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- New hotkey registrations are impossible without an `ActionId`.
- Default shortcuts are discoverable from the registry.
- Inputs/textareas/contenteditable do not trigger global shortcuts.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.3","title":"Help overlay: render from ActionRegistry (no hardcoded shortcuts)","description":"## Goal
Refactor the web help overlay so it *always* reflects current shortcuts.

## Requirements
- Render shortcuts from ActionRegistry (not hardcoded arrays).
- Organize into categories matching the Depth Layers philosophy:
  - Layer 0: sacred surface
  - Layer 1: quick overlays
  - Layer 2+: advanced (analysis menu, simulations, command palette)
- Support “contextual” actions (only show when relevant).

## Acceptance criteria
- Help overlay shows correct shortcuts immediately after keybinding changes.
- No duplicate/conflicting entries.
- UX: clear labeling of scope/context; consistent typography.

## Related
Follow-up/regression hardening for `phage_explorer-m31q` (dynamic hotkey reference).

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.4","title":"Hotkey conflict detection (registry-based)","description":"## Goal
Prevent accidental hotkey conflicts as the app grows.

## Background
A hotkey conflict detector existed in earlier polish work (see `phage_explorer-vss3` / `phage_explorer-ejjg`, closed). The current need is to ensure conflict detection is **powered by the ActionRegistry** so the registry remains the single source of truth.

## Approach
- Add a validator that:
  - scans ActionRegistry shortcuts
  - flags duplicates for the same scope/context
  - optionally flags conflicts with browser-reserved combos
- Run validator at startup in dev/test (not necessarily prod).

## Acceptance criteria
- A test fails if a conflicting shortcut is introduced.
- The validation output clearly points to the conflicting ActionIds.
- The validator checks the registry (not scattered ad-hoc hotkey lists).
